quoteTime: about three o’clock in the afternoon
quoteLast: Satan inspired me to go out on the porch, to survey the prospect.  Immediately I smelled smoke.
title: A winter of content
author: Davidson, Laura Lee

quoteFirst: to N.W. No wind affected it, but without in the least altering its shape, which was arched like a bow, it gradually faded away
quoteLast: Could this bank have been over any inland waters?
title:
Narrative of an Expedition into Central Australia
Performed Under the Authority of Her Majesty's Government, During the Years 1844, 5, and 6, Together With A Notice of the Province of South Australia in 1847
author: Sturt, Charles, 1795-1869

quoteTime: nearly three o'clock in the afternoon
quoteLast: when the steward came to tell him that they were on the point of arriving at their destination. The wharf where they were to land was within sight.
title: Fenton's Quest
author: Braddon, M. E. (Mary Elizabeth), 1835-1915

quoteFirst: Ever since we left the clearing from which the start was made, we had turned our backs on the river, but
quoteTime: about three o'clock in the afternoon
quoteLast: we came suddenly on it again, and stood on the most beautiful spot I ever saw in my life. We were on the top of a high precipice, densely wooded to the water's edge.
title: Station Life in New Zealand
author: Barker, Lady (Mary Anne), 1831-1911

quoteFirst: Sister Peggy always says that little girls should be seen and not heard, and sister Peggy knows--Where was I--Oh! when your grandmother grew old enough to invite her little friends to share her hospitality, the boys and girls would arrive at
quoteTime: about three o'clock in the afternoon
quoteLast: . The girls wore plain print gowns, and muslin aprons edged with tambour work. Instead of that insane mop of hair you sport, with a bow in the middle, looking for the world and all like your terrier, Fluff, they had decent mob caps.
title: The Old-Fashioned Fairy Book
author: Harrison, Burton, Mrs., 1843-1920; Sherwood, Rosina Emmet, 1854-1948 [Illustrator]

quoteLast: , we saw an island, which at first we imagined to be Botel Tobago {317} Xima, but on our nearer approach we found it to be much smaller than that is usually represented; and about an hour after we saw another island, five or six miles farther to the westward.
title: A voyage round the world in the years MDCCXL, I, II, III, IV
author: Walter, Richard, 1716?-1785 [Compiler]; Anson, George Anson, Baron, 1697-1762; Masefield, John, 1878-1967 [Author of introduction, etc.]

quoteFirst: Comes in from Bristol through Bath, Bradford, Trowbridge, Devizes, Westbury, Warminster, Heytesbury, Wells, Shepton Mallet, Frome, etc., etc., Monday about Seven at Night; and Wednesday and Friday,
title:
The King's Post
Being a volume of historical facts relating to the posts, mail coaches, coach roads, and railway mail services of and connected with the ancient city of Bristol from 1580 to the present time
author: Tombs, Robert Charles, 1842-1923

quoteFirst: He shook his Head at Sir _Philip_, and without speaking one Word, left ’em, and hurry’d to _Lucy_, to lament the ill Treatment he had met with from _Friendly_. They coo’d and bill’d as long as he was able; she (sweet Hypocrite) seeming to bemoan his Misfortunes; which he took so kindly, that when he left her, which was
quoteLast: , he caus’d a Scrivener to draw up an Instrument, wherein he settled a hundred Pounds a Year on _Lucy_ for her Life, and gave her a hundred Guineas more against her Lying-in: (For she told him, and indeed ’twas true, that she was with child, and knew her self to be so from a very good Reason--) And indeed she was so--by the _Friendly_ Knight.
title: The Works of Aphra Behn, Volume V
author: Behn, Aphra, 1640-1689; Summers, Montague, 1880-1948 [Editor]

quoteLast: , to my great joy, we arrived at the village of Agath. Our route was over a bare level plain, and our progress like at sea, when the masts of the ship are first seen, then the hull; so here we first saw the heads of the date-palms, then their trunks, and then the clusters of the hovels of the village.
title: Travels in the Great Desert of Sahara, in the Years of 1845 and 1846
author: Richardson, James, 1806-1851

quoteFirst: The island of Newark-Farm is distant only from three to four miles from the mouth of the Elbe; the houses and cattle were now pleasing objects in view.
quoteTime: About three o'clock in the afternoon
quoteLast: we arrived at Cuxhaven, which is a small sea-port town, and is in the territory belonging to the town of Hamburgh. It is a very fashionable bathing spot: and a large concourse of ladies and gentlemen assembled at the haven when the steam packet neared the shore.
title:
Journal of a Horticultural Tour through Germany, Belgium, and part of France, in the Autumn of 1835
To which is added, a Catalogue of the different Species of Cacteæ in the Gardens at Woburn Abbey.
author: Forbes, James, 1773-1861
